preview_text:
The model favors Belgium at 54% to win, with Egypt positioned as a clear underdog at 17%. Belgium's advantage is solid but not dominant; the model gives the Egyptians meaningful odds of pulling an upset.

A draw at 29% emerges as the second-most-likely outcome in the model's forecast, suggesting the teams are competitive enough that a stalemate is plausible. The 20-percentage-point gap between a Belgium win and an Egypt win indicates asymmetry, yet the relatively high draw probability signals this is not a heavily lopsided contest.

The sportsbook consensus shifts toward Belgium, pricing a win at 60%—roughly 6 percentage points higher than the model. The books also compress the draw to 24%, tightening the expected outcome distribution. Polymarket prices align closely with the sportsbooks, leaning further toward Belgium at 61%. The dashboard does not adjudicate between the model and market views; the gap reflects genuine disagreement about Belgium's true advantage.
